intent: Deep / many watches (catalog #39): 20 single-file watches on long deep paths + one recursive /etc/security/ directory watch. Parser handles long paths; ranking must put the recursive dir watch above all 20 file watches; path depth is not a volume signal.

rules.note: RECURSIVE dir watch (f3 section 2.2); dominates. 900 B/event.

rules.line: -w /etc/security/limits.d/99-custom-nofile.conf -p wa -k deepwatch_0 (representative of 20 deep file watches)
rules.note: one of 20 single-file write/attr watches on deep paths; LOW each, 900 B. Path depth is not a volume signal.

suspected_trap: Two traps. (1) The one RECURSIVE /etc/security/ directory watch (recursive subtree, f3 section 2.2) is HIGH and out-weighs all 20 single-file watches combined (~$2.74/mo vs ~$0.55/mo); a model that treats a directory watch as a single path (ignoring recursion) under-ranks it. (2) Path DEPTH is NOT a volume signal: a deep nested path does not generate more events than a shallow one. A model that weights by path length or component count mis-ranks. Correct ranking: recursive dir watch first, then the file watches collectively minor. Parser must handle the long paths without truncation.
